column1: # Threat Intelligence Indicator Feed
# Source: James Brine STIX dataset (converted from STIX 2.x to CSV)
# Description: This dataset contains cyber threat indicators extracted from STIX Indicator objects.
# Each row represents a single indicator associated with malicious activity such as brute-force attacks.
# Column Descriptions:
# id            : Internal numeric identifier for the indicator record generated during the STIX-to-CSV conversion process.
# indicator_id  : STIX Indicator Object ID (format: indicator--UUID). This uniquely identifies the indicator in the STIX dataset.
# indicator     : The observable value extracted from the STIX pattern. In this dataset it represents an IPv4 address used in malicious activity.
# valid_from    : Timestamp indicating when the indicator became valid or was first observed in threat intelligence.
# created       : Timestamp when the STIX indicator object was originally created.
# modified      : Timestamp when the STIX indicator object was last modified or updated.
# name          : Human-readable label describing the threat activity associated with the indicator.
# Timestamp Format:
# All timestamps follow the format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS (UTC unless otherwise specified).
# Indicator Type:
# Most indicators in this dataset represent IPv4 addresses associated with brute-force attacks
# such as SSH or NTP service scanning.
# Data Origin:
# Converted from STIX 2.x objects into CSV format for easier querying
# Note:
# Duplicate indicators may exist if the same observable was reported at different times or associated
# with different STIX objects.
id
